title: Number columns drop the trailing zero on currency
excerpt: Am I wrong? Does the number column really drop the trailing zero from currency amounts.  It changes $543.20 to $543.2.  It does this on every currency field and the formulas using them.

posts.author: mharding7va (Michael Harding)
posts.cookedHtml: Am I wrong? Does the number column really drop the trailing zero from currency amounts. It changes $543.20 to $543.2. It does this on every currency field and the formulas using them.

posts.author: eshin922 (Ed Shin)
posts.cookedHtml: Need an answer to this from Monday. Having the exact same issue.

posts.author: zkendall (Zack Kendall)
posts.cookedHtml: Would like to know about this as well.

posts.author: Karen.Evans (Karen)
posts.cookedHtml: This has been open a while! I’d like to know the answer as well! Trailing zeros shouldn’t be dropped from currency amounts!

posts.author: SYNrtucker (Rae Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. I set the currency setting in the column summary for both number and formula fields and all it does is stick a dollar sign at the front of a shortened number. If currently is selected, it should auto-format to include both decimal places.
